title: Fix Dashboard Status Sync — Active Directives Not Showing

output.summary: Bug fix in directive-watcher status filtering. Touches server watcher + MCP tool consistency. No revenue/auth impact.

Read directive-watcher.ts, server/index.ts, MCP status.ts, types.ts. Root cause: mapStatus() maps 'active' to 'pending' (default case), readCurrentState() hardcodes 'in_progress'/'awaiting_completion' filter.

Read vision.md, lessons/state-management.md, directive-json schema. Schema defines valid statuses as pending/triaged/in_progress/awaiting_completion/completed/failed/reopened — 'active' is NOT valid but used in practice.
1 project, 4 tasks: fix mapStatus() + readCurrentState filter, fix state-watcher mapDirectiveStatus, normalize directive data files, add predev cleanup script.

Confirmed root cause + found additional issue in state-watcher.ts mapDirectiveStatus(). Also found 'superseded' status not handled. Risk: near zero — read-only mapping bug, adding switch cases cannot break existing cases.
Tasks decomposed with DOD. All tasks in one project — single engineer, same files.
Working on current branch (game). No worktree needed.

All 4 tasks completed. TypeScript compiles, Vite build passes. Fixed mapStatus + readCurrentState in directive-watcher, mapDirectiveStatus in state-watcher, normalized 2 directive data files, added predev cleanup script.

All changes verified: mapStatus handles active/reopened/superseded, readCurrentState uses inclusion set, dead reopened removed from post-map filters, state-watcher fixed, data files normalized, predev script added. tsc + vite build pass.
No OKRs to update. Lessons: status mapping functions must handle all schema statuses + common aliases at the boundary.
